chief_complaint: A 55-year-old male patient presents with repeated acute coronary syndromes.
history: Patient with classic cardiovascular risk factors under treatment and apparently well controlled, with good adherence to the treatment, but with repeated acute coronary syndromes. Undiagnosed sleep apnoea-hypopnoea syndrome and atherogenic dyslipidaemia were identified.
exam: Vital signs: BP 140/90 mmHg, HR 70 bpm, RR 16/min. Physical examination: unremarkable.
labs: ECG: ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction. Bloods: elevated lipoprotein (a).
